tier_remaining_note: Tier A remains fully cleared (0). Tier B recomputed from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model on 2026-07-22 (ROUTES_DIR fixed locally to the non-Windows path for this run only, the script file on disk still has the Windows-style path and needs a permanent fix): 283 remaining before chunk 93, minus the 50 Tier B pairs chunk 93 built, leaves 233. Not yet covered by verify_build_state.py's automatic disk reconciliation (only route_pairs/blog/total_markdown_files are); re-run the generator manually each chunk until that gap is closed.
notes: 2026-07-22 Semrush audit fixes: cleared all 45 errors (country BreadcrumbList empty name via country_name/destination_country fallback + jsonify|safeJS; 19 duplicate titles made unique) and the Multiple-H1 warning on about/contact/terms/privacy. No page counts changed. See CLAUDE.md SEMRUSH GUARDRAILS. Chunk 93 DONE (Template C, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 6 parallel research agents, one per cluster, taking the 50 highest-scored unbuilt Tier B pairs from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model (6,921 built, Tier A=0, Tier B=283 remaining before this chunk). Canada-reciprocal cluster (12 routes): closed the reverse legs chunk 92 deliberately deferred for Kyrgyzstan, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Mozambique, North Macedonia, Senegal, Tajikistan and Turkmenistan. CFIA's high-risk-for-dog-rabies list (13 March 2025) was checked individually per country rather than assumed: 10 of the 12 are high-risk (Kyrgyzstan, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Moldova, Mongolia, Mozambique, Senegal, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan), only Montenegro and North Macedonia are not, a much higher high-risk proportion than the batch chunk 92 checked, cross-verified against each country's independent CDC status for confidence. CFIA's 2022 notice still only bans commercial dog imports from high-risk countries, leaving personal pets on a lighter pathway. Iceland-Ireland reciprocal (2 routes): Iceland's Annex II Part 1 listing skips the titre test but not Ireland's tapeworm-treatment requirement, and Iceland's mandatory 14-day MAST quarantine applies regardless of origin. Antigua and Barbuda cluster (3 routes): confirmed DAFF Group 3 approved (same tier as Jamaica), a direct-export case rather than the staged-country pattern used for non-approved countries in earlier chunks; MOCCAE's classification of Antigua and Barbuda could not be confirmed from any published source, flagged as genuine uncertainty and planned conservatively on the titre-test pathway rather than guessed. Singapore reciprocal cluster (20 routes, 10 countries: Armenia, Azerbaijan, Brunei, Georgia, Laos, Panama, Seychelles, Trinidad and Tobago, Uruguay, Uzbekistan): verified AVS Singapore has moved to the Schedule I/II/III system (confirmed live against avs.nparks.gov.sg); none of the 10 countries sit on Schedule I or II, so all default to Schedule III. Genuine differentiators: Azerbaijan Airlines has no Singapore route (transfer via Istanbul/Dubai); Scoot's daily nonstop Singapore-Vientiane flight refuses all live animals, forcing a Bangkok routing on Laos legs both directions; Brunei's own DAA treats Singapore as a scheduled country and can waive the titre test for Singapore-resident pets of six months or more, an asymmetry against Singapore's own Schedule III treatment of Brunei; Trinidad and Tobago's APHD places Singapore in its lightest Category 1, skipping the titre test outbound while the reverse leg needs the full Schedule III sequence; Panama's health certificate needs a stamp from Panama's own Singapore embassy before its aviation authority MIDA will accept it. France cluster (5 routes, one direction: France to Brunei, Laos, Seychelles, Trinidad and Tobago, Uzbekistan): reused each destination's already-verified import side from existing sibling pages, researched France's own export side and airline routing fresh; found Uzbekistan Airways flies Paris CDG-Tashkent nonstop twice weekly (flight HY252), contradicting an assumption in the existing reverse-leg page that no Paris route exists, and that Air France's advertised Paris-Hanoi service is actually a Vietnam Airlines codeshare. Macau cluster (8 routes, brand new pairs: Netherlands, New Zealand, Switzerland, China, both directions): first-ever coverage for these pairs. Macau's own IAM authority process reused from existing Macau sibling pages. Macau's presence on GACC's and MPI's designated/category lists came only from secondary sourcing since primary GACC/MPI pages on Macau specifically were unavailable, flagged as genuine uncertainty rather than assumed; KLM's Amsterdam-Hong Kong service uses a Boeing 787, an aircraft type KLM's own policy bars from carrying hold pets, so Cathay Pacific is the practical carrier despite KLM's own network reaching the corridor. QA pass after all 6 agents finished: zero em dashes, en dashes or curly quotes across all 50 files; zero banned vocabulary (two hits caught and fixed: 'unlocks' rewritten to 'triggers' in a timeline_steps field, 'Streamlined' rewritten to 'Simple' in a title); 20 of 50 files initially used an incorrect template_variant (leftover from a prior chunk's rotation) and were corrected to the assigned C; all 50 initial meta descriptions exceeded the ~160-character budget (up to 294 characters) and were rewritten to fit while preserving each file's core differentiating fact; correct Marcus Webb persona on all 50; every export_requirements/import_requirements block verified as a keyed YAML map; route_complexity restricted to the valid low/moderate/high/very_high tokens; zero corpus-wide title/description collisions checked against the full 6,971-file corpus; zero broken sideways/upward links, each verified against files confirmed to exist on disk; no PetTransportGlobal contact email/phone found on any file (foreign regulatory-authority contact details, e.g. Iceland's MAST and Antigua's Veterinary and Livestock Division, were kept as legitimate sourced regulatory facts, consistent with existing published sibling pages). Routes 6,921 -> 6,971, pages 7,727 -> 7,777. Chunk 94 (Template D, Tier B) is next.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
_previous_notes_chunk_92: Chunk 92 DONE (Template B, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 8 parallel research agents, one per cluster, taking the 50 highest-scored unbuilt Tier B pairs from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model (6,871 built, Tier A=0, Tier B=333 remaining before this chunk). Chunk-91-completion cluster (8 routes): united-kingdom-to-sudan.md (Sudan's own import side freshly researched; Qatar Airways' new Doha-Port Sudan service, 3x/week from 2 July 2026, is a genuinely new gateway distinct from Khartoum) plus united-states-to-X for the 7 countries chunk 91 left one-direction-only (Antigua and Barbuda, Gabon, Grenada, Libya, Papua New Guinea, Saint Lucia, Sudan), reusing each country's already-verified export side and researching the US import side and Sudan's own import side fresh. Iceland reciprocal cluster (8 routes): both directions with Netherlands, New Zealand, Switzerland and China. Iceland confirmed on New Zealand's MPI recognised rabies-free list (same tier as Japan/Singapore/Hawaii) and on GACC's China rabies-free list, but absent from New Zealand's and China's own reverse-direction recognition, a genuine asymmetry; Iceland's mandatory 14-day MAST quarantine (any origin, confirmed still in force) applies regardless of Category 1 status. Macau-South Korea reciprocal cluster (2 routes, brand new pair): Korean Air and Jeju Air both fly Macau-Incheon nonstop, unusually direct for a Macau route, but Korean Air's A321neo on that route is excluded from carrying cargo-hold pets under its own policy, so only cabin-sized pets can use it. Canada cluster (32 routes, the bulk of this chunk): closed out Canada as the only missing destination/origin for 21 countries that already had full UK/US/UAE/Australia coverage (Albania, Algeria, Angola, Bahamas, Belarus, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cameroon, Eswatini, Ivory Coast, Kosovo each both directions; Kyrgyzstan, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Mozambique, North Macedonia, Senegal, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan outbound-from-Canada only, reverse legs deferred to a future chunk). CFIA's high-risk-for-dog-rabies list (verified live against inspection.canada.ca, dated 13 March 2025) was checked individually per country rather than assumed as a block: Albania, Bahamas, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Kosovo confirmed NOT high-risk; Algeria, Angola, Belarus, Cameroon, Eswatini, Ivory Coast confirmed high-risk. Critically, CFIA's own 28 September 2022 notice text was verified to ban only commercial dog imports from high-risk countries, explicitly leaving personal pets out (CFIA has only said it would 'explore options' to tighten personal-pet rules later, not that it has), a materially lighter system than the CDC's titre-test/six-facility-airport machinery used for the US and a genuine differentiator against several already-published Canada sibling pages that don't mention their own destination's high-risk status at all. Confirmed CFIA has no DAFF-style approved-country-group or residency-staging system at all, a real structural difference from Australia's 180-day rule that several of these countries' existing Australia pages describe. Belarus surfaced its own distinct airspace-ban timeline: Canada banned Belarusian aircraft in March 2022 (tied to the Russia invasion), a separate and earlier trigger from the EU/UK's May 2021 Ryanair-incident ban, same practical routing outcome. Montenegro confirmed to apply its titre test with no origin exemption for Canada either, consistent with the asymmetry flagged in earlier chunks. Canada's own Annex II Part 2 EU listing was verified to extend Moldova's and North Macedonia's titre exemptions to Canada on its own merits, not borrowed from the UK/EU relationship. One fabrication risk caught and corrected mid-draft: an agent initially wrote an unsourced 'Turkish Airlines refuses pets on UK-connected flights' claim onto a Canada page with no Canada-specific basis, rewritten to an honest 'unconfirmed, verify directly' framing before the file was finalised. QA pass after all 8 agents finished: zero em dashes, en dashes or curly quotes across all 50 files (verified with a Python Unicode scan); zero banned vocabulary (four watch-list substring hits manually confirmed harmless: 'fostering' inside CFIA's own commercial-dog definition, 'embarkation'/'disembarkation' aviation terms, and the 'PetEmbark' brand name, consistent with the same false-positive pattern logged in chunks 84, 87 and 89); correct Marcus Webb persona and template_variant B on all 50; every export_requirements/import_requirements block verified as a keyed YAML map via a Python parser; route_complexity restricted to the valid low/moderate/high/very_high tokens; zero corpus-wide title/description collisions checked against the full 6,921-file corpus; zero broken sideways/upward links, each verified against files confirmed to exist on disk before inclusion; no PetTransportGlobal contact email/phone found on any file. Routes 6,871 -> 6,921, pages 7,677 -> 7,727. Chunk 93 (Template C, Tier B) is next.
_previous_notes_chunk_91: Chunk 91 DONE (Template A, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 7 parallel research agents, one per cluster, taking the 50 highest-scored unbuilt Tier B pairs from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model (6,821 built, Tier A=0, Tier B=383 remaining before this chunk). UAE outbound cluster (17 routes), Germany outbound cluster (5 routes), Singapore reciprocal cluster (8 routes: corrected Singapore's AVS Category A/B/C/D naming to the current Schedule I/II/III system, confirmed live 21 July 2026), three brand-new-country clusters (20 routes: Antigua and Barbuda, Gabon, Grenada, Libya, Papua New Guinea, Saint Lucia, Sudan to/from UK+US, with UK-to-Sudan and US-reverse-legs deliberately left for chunk 92). CDC high-risk and UK Part 1/Part 2 status independently verified per country. Honest uncertainty flagged on Libya's and Sudan's export processes given their administrative situations. Routes 6,821 -> 6,871, pages 7,627 -> 7,677. Chunk 92 (Template B, Tier B) was next.
_dropped_from_rolling_window: Chunks 80, 81, 82-83, 84, 85-86, 87, 88, 89 and 90 notes were dropped to cap file growth (git history still has them in full at commits 4ecac2f1, 3741f752, ef4d776e, ca50c52f, bd68e3c5, 0cac6bc3, 6bb8b070, 0e8acb9b and d237bd1d).
